rationale: The citing paper uses Harmeling et al. (2021) in its core conceptual setup, not just as a generic background mention. It cites that work to support a key premise that stigmatized consumers are especially attentive to marketplace cues signaling whether they are valued or devalued, which directly feeds into the paper's hypotheses about discounts as disrespect cues. This citation functions in theory building for the mechanism and predictions, even though the citing paper does not reuse Harmeling et al.'s specific methods.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Harmeling et al. as conceptual grounding that stigmatized consumers interpret marketing cues through a threat/value lens. It then builds on that foundation by proposing and testing price discounts on stigma-linked products as a new cue that signals devaluation and triggers perceived disrespect.
